description: Data loaded from CSV file: 75.csv (Dataset: Students Performance in Exams)

fields.name: gender
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field records the self-identified gender of each student, indicating whether they are female or male in the context of exam performance analysis.

fields.name: race/ethnicity
fields.sample_values: group A, group B, group C, group D, group E
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field categorizes students into five demographic groups based on their race or ethnicity, likely for analyzing performance differences across these segments in educational assessments.

fields.name: parental level of education
fields.sample_values: associate's degree, bachelor's degree, high school, master's degree, some college, some high school
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field records the highest educational attainment of a student's parent or guardian, reflecting their formal schooling level, which may be used to analyze its influence on student academic performance.

fields.name: lunch
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field indicates whether a student receives a free or reduced-price lunch (suggesting lower socioeconomic status) or a standard lunch, typically used to assess the impact of socioeconomic factors on academic performance.

fields.name: test preparation course
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field indicates whether a student has completed a test preparation course, reflecting their participation in exam-related academic support programs.

fields.name: math score
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents each student's numerical score (ranging from 0 to 100) achieved in a math exam, indicating their performance or proficiency in mathematics within an academic assessment context.

fields.name: reading score
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: The "reading score" field represents a student's numerical score (from 17 to 100) on a reading assessment, indicating their proficiency in reading-related skills within an academic exam context.

fields.name: writing score
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: The "writing score" field represents each student's numerical performance result (on a scale of 10 to 100) in the writing section of an academic exam, reflecting their proficiency in written communication skills.

formatted_summary:
## Table 1: Students_Performance_in_Exams (1,000 rows × 8 columns)

### Description
Data loaded from CSV file: 75.csv (Dataset: Students Performance in Exams)

### Schema (8 fields)
  - gender -- type: object, values: female, male
  - race/ethnicity -- type: object, values: group A, group B, group C, group D, group E
  - parental level of education -- type: object, values: associate's degree, bachelor's degree, high school, master's degree, some college, some high school
  - lunch -- type: object, values: free/reduced, standard
  - test preparation course -- type: object, values: completed, none
  - math score -- type: int64, values: 0, 8, 18, 19, 22, ..., 96, 97, 98, 99, 100
  - reading score -- type: int64, values: 17, 23, 24, 26, 28, ..., 95, 96, 97, 99, 100
  - writing score -- type: int64, values: 10, 15, 19, 22, 23, ..., 96, 97, 98, 99, 100

### Sample Data (first 5 rows)
```
   gender race/ethnicity parental level of education         lunch test preparation course  math score  reading score  writing score
0  female        group B           bachelor's degree      standard                    none          72             72             74
1  female        group C                some college      standard               completed          69             90             88
2  female        group B             master's degree      standard                    none          90             95             93
3    male        group A          associate's degree  free/reduced                    none          47             57             44
4    male        group C                some college      standard                    none          76             78             75
```
